lede: A look at July 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including George W. Bush and Daniel Morgan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Boeing 777
events.desc: A Boeing 777 operating as Asiana Airlines Flight 214 crashes at San Francisco International Airport, killing three and injuring 181 of the 307 people on board.

events.title: Mamudo school massacre
events.desc: At least 42 people are killed in a shooting at a school in Yobe State, Nigeria.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: In response to the Drumcree dispute, five days of mass protests, riots and gun battles begin in Irish nationalist districts of Northern Ireland.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German army launches its offensive to encircle several Soviet armies near Smolensk.

events.title: Story Bridge
events.desc: Story Bridge, a major landmark in Brisbane, as well as Australia's longest cantilever bridge is formally opened.

events.title: Manchester, Bolton and Bury Canal
events.desc: A major breach of the Manchester, Bolton and Bury Canal in England sends millions of gallons of water cascading 200 feet (61 m) into the River Irwell.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Arabian troops led by T. E. Lawrence ("Lawrence of Arabia") and Auda ibu Tayi capture Aqaba from the Ottoman Empire during the Arab Revolt.

events.title: Louis Pasteur
events.desc: Louis Pasteur successfully tests his vaccine against rabies on Joseph Meister, a boy who was bitten by a rabid dog.

events.title: Padua
events.desc: At Padua, the Emperor Leopold II calls on the monarchs of Europe to join him in demanding the king of France Louis XVI's freedom.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Siege of Fort Ticonderoga: After a bombardment by British artillery under General John Burgoyne, American forces retreat from Fort Ticonderoga, New York.

births.name: George W. Bush
births.line: American businessman and politician, 43rd President of the United States

deaths.text: Ennio Morricone, Italian composer, orchestrator, conductor, and trumpet player
Zhu De, Chinese general and politician, Chairman of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress
Louis Armstrong, American singer and trumpet player
Guy de Maupassant, French short story writer, novelist, and poet

observances.text: The first day of the Festival of San Fermín, which lasts until July 14. (Pamplona)
Independence Day (Comoros), celebrates the independence of the Comoros from France in 1975.
Independence Day (Malawi), celebrates the independence of Malawi from United Kingdom in 1964.
